title: For An Epitaph At Fiesole
content:
Lo! where the four mimosas blend their shade
In calm repose at last is Landor laid;
For ere he slept he saw them planted here
By her his soul had ever held most dear,
And he had liv�d enough when he had dried her tear.
meaning:
Walter Savage Landor's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "For An Epitaph At Fiesole"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
